40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Prisma Generator System - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the monorepo, here's the complete breakdown of how Prisma generators work:

---

### 1. **`generatorHandler` API Signature**

**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

**Exact Signature:**
```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

**How it Works:**
- `generatorHandler` accepts a `Handler` object with two async callback methods
- It reads JSON-RPC 2.0 messages from `process.stdin` (one per line)
- Handles two methods:
  - `generate` method → calls `handler.onGenerate(params)` 
  - `getManifest` method → calls `handler.onManifest(params)`
- Responses are written to `process.stderr` as JSON-RPC responses
- Communication uses message IDs for request/response correlation
- Errors are caught and returned as JSON-RPC error responses with stack traces

---

### 2. **GeneratorOptions Type Structure**

**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}

export interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: { [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined }
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: { models?: string[]; fields?: string[] }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

---

### 3. **DMMF Structure**

**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

**Key Datamodel Types:**

```typescript
export type Document = {
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}

export type Datamodel = {
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}

export type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}

export type Field = {
  kind: FieldKind // 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string // BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
```

---

### 4. **Package.json Patterns**

**Generator Package Naming Convention:**
- Pattern: `@prisma/client-generator-js` or `@prisma/client-generator-ts`
- Alternative: `@prisma/generator-helper` (for the protocol library)

**Dependency Pattern (from client generators):**
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-js",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/client-common": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/debug": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

---

### 5. **tsconfig Pattern**

**tsconfig.json:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**tsconfig.build.json:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

**Root tsconfig.build.regular.json** sets:
- `target: "ES2022"`
- `module: "commonjs"`
- `emitDeclarationOnly: true`
- `sourceMap: true`
- `strict: true`

---

### 6. **Build Configuration Pattern**

**helpers/build.ts Pattern:**
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

**helpers/compile/configs.ts:**
```typescript
export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([
  { entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }
])

// Generates both CJS and ESM builds:
// - dist/index.js (CommonJS)
// - dist/index.mjs (ES Module)
// - dist/index.d.ts (types)
// - dist/index.d.mts (ESM types)
```

**Build Features:**
- Bundles entry point into single file (CJS + ESM)
- Emits TypeScript declaration files
- Uses esbuild with plugins for compilation
- Platform: `node`, Target: `ES2022`
- External dependencies marked from `package.json`

---

### 7. **Generator Implementation Pattern**

**Location:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'

export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'

  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const { config } = options.generator
    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
    
    // Access DMMF models:
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.models (array of Model)
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.enums
    // options.dmmf.schema (for GraphQL schema types)
    // options.datasources (array of DataSource)
    
    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      outputDir,
      // ... other options
    })
  }
}
```

---

### 8. **Entry Point Pattern (JSON-RPC Adapter)**

**Location:** `/packages/client/src/generation/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'

if (process.argv[1] === __filename) {
  const generator = new PrismaClientJsGenerator()

  generatorHandler({
    onManifest(config) {
      return generator.getManifest(config)
    },
    onGenerate(options) {
      return generator.generate(options)
    },
  })
}
```

---

### 9. **Real-World Example (Minimal Generator)**

**From test fixtures** (`/packages/cli/src/__tests__/fixtures/dependent-generator/generator.js`):

```javascript
#!/usr/bin/node

const { generatorHandler } = require('@prisma/generator-helper')

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: 'my-generator-output',
      prettyName: 'I depend on the client',
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresGenerators: ['prisma-client-js'],
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    // Access DMMF:
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.models
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.enums
    // options.generator (config)
    // options.datasources
    // options.schemaPath
  },
})
```

---

### 10. **File Structure for New Generator Package**

```
packages/my-generator/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Exports public API
│   ├── generator.ts      # Generator class implementing Generator interface
│   └── ...               # Implementation files
└── tests/
    └── generator.test.ts
```

---

### Key Patterns Summary

1. **Generator Pattern:** Implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`
2. **Registration:** Wrap with `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper` 
3. **DMMF Access:** Via `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` and `options.dmmf.schema`
4. **Configuration:** Read from `options.generator.config` and `options.generator.output`
5. **Async Operations:** Both `getManifest()` and `generate()` are async
6. **Build Process:** Use esbuild via `helpers/build.ts` → `helpers/compile/build.ts`
7. **Module Format:** Dual CJS+ESM with TypeScript declarations
8. **Dependencies:** Mark `@prisma/*` deps as `workspace:*`

---

**Key Files Reviewed:**
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` - Protocol implementation
- `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` - Type definitions
- `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` - DMMF types
- `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts` - Reference implementation
- `/packages/client-generator-js/package.json` - Dependency patterns
- `/helpers/compile/configs.ts` - Build configuration
- `/helpers/compile/build.ts` - Build execution

```json
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**tsconfig.build.json:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

**Root tsconfig.build.regular.json** sets:
- `target: "ES2022"`
- `module: "commonjs"`
- `emitDeclarationOnly: true`
- `sourceMap: true`
- `strict: true`

---

### 6. **Build Configuration Pattern**

**helpers/build.ts Pattern:**
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

**helpers/compile/configs.ts:**
```typescript
export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([
  { entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }
])

// Generates both CJS and ESM builds:
// - dist/index.js (CommonJS)
// - dist/index.mjs (ES Module)
// - dist/index.d.ts (types)
// - dist/index.d.mts (ESM types)
```

**Build Features:**
- Bundles entry point into single file (CJS + ESM)
- Emits TypeScript declaration files
- Uses esbuild with plugins for compilation
- Platform: `node`, Target: `ES2022`
- External dependencies marked from `package.json`

---

### 7. **Generator Implementation Pattern**

**Location:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'

export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'

  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return Promise.resolve({
      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    })
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const { config } = options.generator
    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
    
    // Access DMMF models:
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.models (array of Model)
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.enums
    // options.dmmf.schema (for GraphQL schema types)
    // options.datasources (array of DataSource)
    
    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      outputDir,
      // ... other options
    })
  }
}
```

---

### 8. **Entry Point Pattern (JSON-RPC Adapter)**

**Location:** `/packages/client/src/generation/generator.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'

if (process.argv[1] === __filename) {
  const generator = new PrismaClientJsGenerator()

  generatorHandler({
    onManifest(config) {
      return generator.getManifest(config)
    },
    onGenerate(options) {
      return generator.generate(options)
    },
  })
}
```

---

### 9. **Real-World Example (Minimal Generator)**

**From test fixtures** (`/packages/cli/src/__tests__/fixtures/dependent-generator/generator.js`):

```javascript
#!/usr/bin/node

const { generatorHandler } = require('@prisma/generator-helper')

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      defaultOutput: 'my-generator-output',
      prettyName: 'I depend on the client',
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresGenerators: ['prisma-client-js'],
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    // Access DMMF:
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.models
    // options.dmmf.datamodel.enums
    // options.generator (config)
    // options.datasources
    // options.schemaPath
  },
})
```

---

### 10. **File Structure for New Generator Package**

```
packages/my-generator/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Exports public API
│   ├── generator.ts      # Generator class implementing Generator interface
│   └── ...               # Implementation files
└── tests/
    └── generator.test.ts
```

---

### Key Patterns Summary

1. **Generator Pattern:** Implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`
2. **Registration:** Wrap with `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper` 
3. **DMMF Access:** Via `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` and `options.dmmf.schema`
4. **Configuration:** Read from `options.generator.config` and `options.generator.output`
5. **Async Operations:** Both `getManifest()` and `generate()` are async
6. **Build Process:** Use esbuild via `helpers/build.ts` → `helpers/compile/build.ts`
7. **Module Format:** Dual CJS+ESM with TypeScript declarations
8. **Dependencies:** Mark `@prisma/*` deps as `workspace:*`

---

**Key Files Reviewed:**
- `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` - Protocol implementation
- `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` - Type definitions
- `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` - DMMF types
- `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts` - Reference implementation
- `/packages/client-generator-js/package.json` - Dependency patterns
- `/helpers/compile/configs.ts` - Build configuration
- `/helpers/compile/build.ts` - Build execution

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

3	import { SqlQueryOutput } from './typedSql'
4	
5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string
7	  output: EnvValue | null
8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81	
82	export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
83	
84	export type GeneratorManifest = {
85	  prettyName?: string
86	  defaultOutput?: string
87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
93	  version?: string

import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
import glob from 'globby'
import path from 'path'

import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
import { map } from '../blaze/map'
import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'

export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
  name?: string
  emitTypes?: boolean
  emitMetafile?: boolean
  outbase?: never // we don't support this
}

const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
  platform: 'node',
  target: 'ES2022',
  logLevel: 'error',
  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
  metafile: true,
} as const

/**
 * Apply defaults to the original build options
 * @param options the original build options
 */
const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
  format: 'cjs',
  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
  }),
  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
  ...options,
  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries

content.text: Let me rewrite `generator.ts` with a cleaner approach - the nested template literals are fragile. I'll use a helper function to build the generated code more robustly:

input.content:
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { docComment, fieldType, fileHeader } from './template'

interface ScalarField {
  name: string
  tsType: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isId: boolean
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  documentation: string | undefined
}

/**
 * Extracts the scalar (non-relation) fields from a DMMF model.
 * Relation fields (kind === 'object') are excluded because the
 * generated REST client deals with flat JSON payloads.
 */
function extractScalarFields(model: DMMF.Model): ScalarField[] {
  return model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
    .map((f) => ({
      name: f.name,
      tsType: fieldType(f.type, f.kind, f.isList, f.isRequired),
      isRequired: f.isRequired,
      isId: f.isId,
      hasDefaultValue: f.hasDefaultValue,
      documentation: f.documentation,
    }))
}

/** Resolves the identifier field name and TypeScript type for a model. */
function resolveIdField(model: DMMF.Model): { name: string; tsType: string } {
  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
  if (idField) {
    return { name: idField.name, tsType: fieldType(idField.type, idField.kind, false, true) }
  }
  if (model.primaryKey && model.primaryKey.fields.length > 0) {
    const pkFieldName = model.primaryKey.fields[0]
    const pkField = model.fields.find((f) => f.name === pkFieldName)
    if (pkField) {
      return { name: pkFieldName, tsType: fieldType(pkField.type, pkField.kind, false, true) }
    }
  }
  return { name: 'id', tsType: 'string | number' }
}

/** Generates the TypeScript interface for a model's full shape. */
function generateModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push(docComment(model.documentation, '') + 'export interface ' + model.name + ' {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push(docComment(field.documentation, '  ') + '  ' + field.name + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/**
 * Generates a create-input type.
 * Fields with default values or that are IDs are made optional.
 */
function generateCreateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'CreateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    const optional = field.hasDefaultValue || field.isId ? '?' : ''
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + optional + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates an update-input type where all fields are optional. */
function generateUpdateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'UpdateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + '?: ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates a WhereUniqueInput type keyed on the model's ID field. */
function generateWhereUniqueInput(modelName: string, idName: string, idTsType: string): string {
  return 'export interface ' + modelName + 'WhereUniqueInput {\n  ' + idName + ': ' + idTsType + '\n}'
}

/**
 * Generates a typed client class with CRUD methods for one model.
 * Uses `fetch` under the hood and returns properly typed responses.
 */
function generateModelClient(model: DMMF.Model, idInfo: { name: string; tsType: string }): string {
  const name = model.name
  const resource = name[0].toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)

  // Build the class as an array of lines for clarity.
  // Template literal syntax in the output is written with escaped backticks.
  const bt = '`' // backtick character used in generated template literals

  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export class ' + name + 'Client {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push('  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {')
  lines.push('    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl')
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findMany
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve all ' + resource + ' records, with optional query parameters. */')
  lines.push('  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<' + name + '[]> {')
  lines.push("    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''")
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + '${query}' + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'GET /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '[]>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findUnique
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve a single ' + resource + ' by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async findUnique(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<' + name + ' | null> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push('    if (response.status === 404) return null')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'GET /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // create
  lines.push('  /** Create a new ' + resource + ' record. */')
  lines.push('  async create(data: ' + name + 'CreateInput): Promise<' + name + '> {')
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'POST',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'POST /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // update
  lines.push('  /** Update an existing ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push(
    '  async update(' +
      idInfo.name +
      ': ' +
      idInfo.tsType +
      ', data: ' +
      name +
      'UpdateInput): Promise<' +
      name +
      '> {',
  )
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'PATCH',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'PATCH /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // delete
  lines.push('  /** Delete a ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async delete(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<void> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'DELETE',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'DELETE /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('  }')

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates the root client class that composes per-model clients. */
function generateRootClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('/** Typed REST API client generated from your Prisma schema. */')
  lines.push('export class RestClient {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push("  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {")
  lines.push("    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/+$/, '')")
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')

  for (const model of models) {
    const resource = model.name[0].toLowerCase() + model.name.slice(1)
    lines.push(
      '  readonly ' + resource + ' = new ' + model.name + 'Client(this.#baseUrl, this.#headers)',
    )
  }

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates TypeScript union type declarations for Prisma enums. */
function generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string {
  return enums
    .map((e) => {
      const values = e.values.map((v) => "  | '" + v.name + "'").join('\n')
      const doc = docComment(e.documentation, '')
      return doc + 'export type ' + e.name + ' =\n' + values
    })
    .join('\n\n')
}

/**
 * Transforms a DMMF document into a complete TypeScript REST client source file.
 */
export function generate(dmmf: DMMF.Document): string {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel
  const sections: string[] = [fileHeader()]

  if (enums.length > 0) {
    sections.push('// ---------- Enums ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateEnums(enums))
    sections.push('')
  }

  for (const model of models) {
    const fields = extractScalarFields(model)
    const idInfo = resolveIdField(model)

    sections.push('// ---------- ' + model.name + ' ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateModelInterface(model, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateCreateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateUpdateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateWhereUniqueInput(model.name, idInfo.name, idInfo.tsType))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateModelClient(model, idInfo))
    sections.push('')
  }

  sections.push('// ---------- Root Client ----------\n')
  sections.push(generateRootClient(models))
  sections.push('')

  return sections.join('\n')
}

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { docComment, fieldType, fileHeader } from './template'

interface ScalarField {
  name: string
  tsType: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isId: boolean
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  documentation: string | undefined
}

/**
 * Extracts the scalar (non-relation) fields from a DMMF model.
 * Relation fields (kind === 'object') are excluded because the
 * generated REST client deals with flat JSON payloads.
 */
function extractScalarFields(model: DMMF.Model): ScalarField[] {
  return model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
    .map((f) => ({
      name: f.name,
      tsType: fieldType(f.type, f.kind, f.isList, f.isRequired),
      isRequired: f.isRequired,
      isId: f.isId,
      hasDefaultValue: f.hasDefaultValue,
      documentation: f.documentation,
    }))
}

/** Resolves the identifier field name and TypeScript type for a model. */
function resolveIdField(model: DMMF.Model): { name: string; tsType: string } {
  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
  if (idField) {
    return { name: idField.name, tsType: fieldType(idField.type, idField.kind, false, true) }
  }
  if (model.primaryKey && model.primaryKey.fields.length > 0) {
    const pkFieldName = model.primaryKey.fields[0]
    const pkField = model.fields.find((f) => f.name === pkFieldName)
    if (pkField) {
      return { name: pkFieldName, tsType: fieldType(pkField.type, pkField.kind, false, true) }
    }
  }
  return { name: 'id', tsType: 'string | number' }
}

/** Generates the TypeScript interface for a model's full shape. */
function generateModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push(docComment(model.documentation, '') + 'export interface ' + model.name + ' {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push(docComment(field.documentation, '  ') + '  ' + field.name + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/**
 * Generates a create-input type.
 * Fields with default values or that are IDs are made optional.
 */
function generateCreateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'CreateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    const optional = field.hasDefaultValue || field.isId ? '?' : ''
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + optional + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates an update-input type where all fields are optional. */
function generateUpdateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'UpdateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + '?: ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates a WhereUniqueInput type keyed on the model's ID field. */
function generateWhereUniqueInput(modelName: string, idName: string, idTsType: string): string {
  return 'export interface ' + modelName + 'WhereUniqueInput {\n  ' + idName + ': ' + idTsType + '\n}'
}

/**
 * Generates a typed client class with CRUD methods for one model.
 * Uses `fetch` under the hood and returns properly typed responses.
 */
function generateModelClient(model: DMMF.Model, idInfo: { name: string; tsType: string }): string {
  const name = model.name
  const resource = name[0].toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)

  // Build the class as an array of lines for clarity.
  // Template literal syntax in the output is written with escaped backticks.
  const bt = '`' // backtick character used in generated template literals

  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export class ' + name + 'Client {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push('  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {')
  lines.push('    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl')
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findMany
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve all ' + resource + ' records, with optional query parameters. */')
  lines.push('  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<' + name + '[]> {')
  lines.push("    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''")
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + '${query}' + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'GET /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '[]>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findUnique
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve a single ' + resource + ' by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async findUnique(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<' + name + ' | null> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push('    if (response.status === 404) return null')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'GET /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // create
  lines.push('  /** Create a new ' + resource + ' record. */')
  lines.push('  async create(data: ' + name + 'CreateInput): Promise<' + name + '> {')
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'POST',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'POST /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // update
  lines.push('  /** Update an existing ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push(
    '  async update(' +
      idInfo.name +
      ': ' +
      idInfo.tsType +
      ', data: ' +
      name +
      'UpdateInput): Promise<' +
      name +
      '> {',
  )
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'PATCH',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'PATCH /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // delete
  lines.push('  /** Delete a ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async delete(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<void> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'DELETE',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'DELETE /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('  }')

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates the root client class that composes per-model clients. */
function generateRootClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('/** Typed REST API client generated from your Prisma schema. */')
  lines.push('export class RestClient {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push("  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {")
  lines.push("    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/+$/, '')")
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')

  for (const model of models) {
    const resource = model.name[0].toLowerCase() + model.name.slice(1)
    lines.push(
      '  readonly ' + resource + ' = new ' + model.name + 'Client(this.#baseUrl, this.#headers)',
    )
  }

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates TypeScript union type declarations for Prisma enums. */
function generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string {
  return enums
    .map((e) => {
      const values = e.values.map((v) => "  | '" + v.name + "'").join('\n')
      const doc = docComment(e.documentation, '')
      return doc + 'export type ' + e.name + ' =\n' + values
    })
    .join('\n\n')
}

/**
 * Transforms a DMMF document into a complete TypeScript REST client source file.
 */
export function generate(dmmf: DMMF.Document): string {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel
  const sections: string[] = [fileHeader()]

  if (enums.length > 0) {
    sections.push('// ---------- Enums ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateEnums(enums))
    sections.push('')
  }

  for (const model of models) {
    const fields = extractScalarFields(model)
    const idInfo = resolveIdField(model)

    sections.push('// ---------- ' + model.name + ' ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateModelInterface(model, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateCreateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateUpdateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateWhereUniqueInput(model.name, idInfo.name, idInfo.tsType))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateModelClient(model, idInfo))
    sections.push('')
  }

  sections.push('// ---------- Root Client ----------\n')
  sections.push(generateRootClient(models))
  sections.push('')

  return sections.join('\n')
}

content.thinking:
The issue is that the `tsconfig.build.json` can't find the workspace packages. This is because the workspace dependencies need to be built first, or I need to set up the tsconfig to reference the source files. Let me look at how the `tsconfig.build.regular.json` works and check if there's a path mapping I need to add.

Actually, looking at the error more carefully - the `tsconfig.build.json` extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json` which doesn't have path mappings. The `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` is the one with path mappings. 

The build process uses `tsconfig.build.json` by default and runs `tsc --project tsconfig.build.json --paths null`, which means it's trying to resolve imports from already-compiled outputs. The real problem is that the workspace dependencies like `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator-helper` need to be built before other packages can reference them. Let me check how other generators handle this same setup.

10	  Json: 'unknown',
11	  Bytes: 'Buffer',
12	}
13	
14	/**
15	 * Resolves a Prisma field type to the corresponding TypeScript type string.
16	 * Relation (object) fields are resolved to the related model's interface name.
17	 * Enum fields are resolved to the enum's name (assumed to be generated alongside).
18	 */
19	export function toTsType(fieldType: string, fieldKind: string): string {
20	  if (fieldKind === 'enum') {
21	    return fieldType
22	  }
23	  if (fieldKind === 'object') {
24	    return fieldType
25	  }
26	  return PRISMA_TO_TS_TYPE[fieldType] ?? 'unknown'
27	}
28	
29	/** Wraps a type string as an array type when the field is a list. */
30	export function wrapList(tsType: string, isList: boolean): string {
31	  if (!isList) return tsType
32	  // Wrap union/complex types in parens for readability
33	  if (tsType.includes('|')) return `(${tsType})[]`
34	  return `${tsType}[]`
35	}
36	
37	/** Appends ` | null` when the field is optional (not required). */
38	export function wrapNullable(tsType: string, isRequired: boolean): string {
39	  if (isRequired) return tsType
40	  return `${tsType} | null`
41	}
42	
43	/** Builds the full TypeScript type annotation for a single field. */
44	export function fieldType(type: string, kind: string, isList: boolean, isRequired: boolean): string {
45	  return wrapNullable(wrapList(toTsType(type, kind), isList), isRequired)
46	}
47	
48	/** Produces a JSDoc block from a documentation string, or empty string if absent. */
49	export function docComment(documentation: string | undefined, indent: string): string {
50	  if (!documentation) return ''
51	  const lines = documentation.split('\n')
52	  if (lines.length === 1) return `${indent}/** ${lines[0]} */\n`
53	  const middle = lines.map((l) => `${indent} * ${l}`).join('\n')
54	  return `${indent}/**\n${middle}\n${indent} */\n`
55	}
56	
57	/** Renders the file header with auto-generation warning. */
58	export function fileHeader(): string {
59	  return [
60	    '// -----------------------------------------------------------------------',
61	    '// This file was generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client.',
62	    '// Do not edit it manually.',
63	    '// -----------------------------------------------------------------------',
64	    '',
65	    '',
66	  ].join('\n')
67	}
68	
69	/** Indents every line of a multi-line string by the given prefix. */
70	export function indent(text: string, prefix = '  '): string {
71	  return text
72	    .split('\n')
73	    .map((line) => (line.trim() === '' ? '' : `${prefix}${line}`))
74	    .join('\n')
75	}

253	/** Generates the root client class that composes per-model clients. */
254	function generateRootClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
255	  const lines: string[] = []
256	  lines.push('/** Typed REST API client generated from your Prisma schema. */')
257	  lines.push('export class RestClient {')
258	  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
259	  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
260	  lines.push('')
261	  lines.push("  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {")
262	  lines.push("    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/+$/, '')")
263	  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
264	  lines.push('  }')
265	
266	  for (const model of models) {
267	    const resource = model.name[0].toLowerCase() + model.name.slice(1)
268	    lines.push(
269	      '  readonly ' + resource + ' = new ' + model.name + 'Client(this.#baseUrl, this.#headers)',
270	    )
271	  }
272	
273	  lines.push('}')
274	  return lines.join('\n')
275	}

277	/** Generates TypeScript union type declarations for Prisma enums. */
278	function generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string {
279	  return enums
280	    .map((e) => {
281	      const values = e.values.map((v) => "  | '" + v.name + "'").join('\n')
282	      const doc = docComment(e.documentation, '')
283	      return doc + 'export type ' + e.name + ' =\n' + values
284	    })
285	    .join('\n\n')
286	}
287	
288	/**
289	 * Transforms a DMMF document into a complete TypeScript REST client source file.
290	 */
291	export function generate(dmmf: DMMF.Document): string {
292	  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel
293	  const sections: string[] = [fileHeader()]
294	
295	  if (enums.length > 0) {
296	    sections.push('// ---------- Enums ----------\n')
297	    sections.push(generateEnums(enums))
298	    sections.push('')
299	  }
300	
301	  for (const model of models) {
302	    const fields = extractScalarFields(model)
303	    const idInfo = resolveIdField(model)
304	
305	    sections.push('// ---------- ' + model.name + ' ----------\n')
306	    sections.push(generateModelInterface(model, fields))
307	    sections.push('')
308	    sections.push(generateCreateInput(model.name, fields))
309	    sections.push('')
310	    sections.push(generateUpdateInput(model.name, fields))
311	    sections.push('')
312	    sections.push(generateWhereUniqueInput(model.name, idInfo.name, idInfo.tsType))
313	    sections.push('')
314	    sections.push(generateModelClient(model, idInfo))
315	    sections.push('')
316	  }
317	
318	  sections.push('// ---------- Root Client ----------\n')
319	  sections.push(generateRootClient(models))
320	  sections.push('')
321	
322	  return sections.join('\n')
323	}

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { docComment, fieldType, fileHeader } from './template'

interface ScalarField {
  name: string
  tsType: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isId: boolean
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  documentation: string | undefined
}

/**
 * Extracts the scalar (non-relation) fields from a DMMF model.
 * Relation fields (kind === 'object') are excluded because the
 * generated REST client deals with flat JSON payloads.
 */
function extractScalarFields(model: DMMF.Model): ScalarField[] {
  return model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
    .map((f) => ({
      name: f.name,
      tsType: fieldType(f.type, f.kind, f.isList, f.isRequired),
      isRequired: f.isRequired,
      isId: f.isId,
      hasDefaultValue: f.hasDefaultValue,
      documentation: f.documentation,
    }))
}

/** Resolves the identifier field name and TypeScript type for a model. */
function resolveIdField(model: DMMF.Model): { name: string; tsType: string } {
  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
  if (idField) {
    return { name: idField.name, tsType: fieldType(idField.type, idField.kind, false, true) }
  }
  if (model.primaryKey && model.primaryKey.fields.length > 0) {
    const pkFieldName = model.primaryKey.fields[0]
    const pkField = model.fields.find((f) => f.name === pkFieldName)
    if (pkField) {
      return { name: pkFieldName, tsType: fieldType(pkField.type, pkField.kind, false, true) }
    }
  }
  return { name: 'id', tsType: 'string | number' }
}

/** Generates the TypeScript interface for a model's full shape. */
function generateModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push(docComment(model.documentation, '') + 'export interface ' + model.name + ' {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push(docComment(field.documentation, '  ') + '  ' + field.name + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/**
 * Generates a create-input type.
 * Fields with default values or that are IDs are made optional.
 */
function generateCreateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'CreateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    const optional = field.hasDefaultValue || field.isId ? '?' : ''
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + optional + ': ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates an update-input type where all fields are optional. */
function generateUpdateInput(modelName: string, fields: ScalarField[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export interface ' + modelName + 'UpdateInput {')
  for (const field of fields) {
    lines.push('  ' + field.name + '?: ' + field.tsType)
  }
  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates a WhereUniqueInput type keyed on the model's ID field. */
function generateWhereUniqueInput(modelName: string, idName: string, idTsType: string): string {
  return 'export interface ' + modelName + 'WhereUniqueInput {\n  ' + idName + ': ' + idTsType + '\n}'
}

/**
 * Generates a typed client class with CRUD methods for one model.
 * Uses `fetch` under the hood and returns properly typed responses.
 */
function generateModelClient(model: DMMF.Model, idInfo: { name: string; tsType: string }): string {
  const name = model.name
  const resource = name[0].toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)

  // Build the class as an array of lines for clarity.
  // Template literal syntax in the output is written with escaped backticks.
  const bt = '`' // backtick character used in generated template literals

  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('export class ' + name + 'Client {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push('  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {')
  lines.push('    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl')
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findMany
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve all ' + resource + ' records, with optional query parameters. */')
  lines.push('  async findMany(params?: Record<string, string>): Promise<' + name + '[]> {')
  lines.push("    const query = params ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(params).toString() : ''")
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + '${query}' + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'GET /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '[]>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // findUnique
  lines.push('  /** Retrieve a single ' + resource + ' by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async findUnique(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<' + name + ' | null> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'GET',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push('    if (response.status === 404) return null')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'GET /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // create
  lines.push('  /** Create a new ' + resource + ' record. */')
  lines.push('  async create(data: ' + name + 'CreateInput): Promise<' + name + '> {')
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(' + bt + '${this.#baseUrl}/' + resource + bt + ', {')
  lines.push("      method: 'POST',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' + bt + 'POST /' + resource + ' failed: ${response.status}' + bt + ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // update
  lines.push('  /** Update an existing ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push(
    '  async update(' +
      idInfo.name +
      ': ' +
      idInfo.tsType +
      ', data: ' +
      name +
      'UpdateInput): Promise<' +
      name +
      '> {',
  )
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'PATCH',")
  lines.push("      headers: { ...this.#headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },")
  lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(data),')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'PATCH /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<' + name + '>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  // delete
  lines.push('  /** Delete a ' + resource + ' record by its unique identifier. */')
  lines.push('  async delete(' + idInfo.name + ': ' + idInfo.tsType + '): Promise<void> {')
  lines.push(
    '    const response = await fetch(' +
      bt +
      '${this.#baseUrl}/' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '}' +
      bt +
      ', {',
  )
  lines.push("      method: 'DELETE',")
  lines.push('      headers: this.#headers,')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push(
    '    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(' +
      bt +
      'DELETE /' +
      resource +
      '/${' +
      idInfo.name +
      '} failed: ${response.status}' +
      bt +
      ')',
  )
  lines.push('  }')

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates the root client class that composes per-model clients. */
function generateRootClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = []
  lines.push('/** Typed REST API client generated from your Prisma schema. */')
  lines.push('export class RestClient {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push("  constructor(baseUrl: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}) {")
  lines.push("    this.#baseUrl = baseUrl.replace(/\\/+$/, '')")
  lines.push('    this.#headers = headers')
  lines.push('  }')

  for (const model of models) {
    const resource = model.name[0].toLowerCase() + model.name.slice(1)
    lines.push(
      '  readonly ' + resource + ' = new ' + model.name + 'Client(this.#baseUrl, this.#headers)',
    )
  }

  lines.push('}')
  return lines.join('\n')
}

/** Generates TypeScript union type declarations for Prisma enums. */
function generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string {
  return enums
    .map((e) => {
      const values = e.values.map((v) => "  | '" + v.name + "'").join('\n')
      const doc = docComment(e.documentation, '')
      return doc + 'export type ' + e.name + ' =\n' + values
    })
    .join('\n\n')
}

/**
 * Transforms a DMMF document into a complete TypeScript REST client source file.
 */
export function generate(dmmf: DMMF.Document): string {
  const { models, enums } = dmmf.datamodel
  const sections: string[] = [fileHeader()]

  if (enums.length > 0) {
    sections.push('// ---------- Enums ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateEnums(enums))
    sections.push('')
  }

  for (const model of models) {
    const fields = extractScalarFields(model)
    const idInfo = resolveIdField(model)

    sections.push('// ---------- ' + model.name + ' ----------\n')
    sections.push(generateModelInterface(model, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateCreateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateUpdateInput(model.name, fields))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateWhereUniqueInput(model.name, idInfo.name, idInfo.tsType))
    sections.push('')
    sections.push(generateModelClient(model, idInfo))
    sections.push('')
  }

  sections.push('// ---------- Root Client ----------\n')
  sections.push(generateRootClient(models))
  sections.push('')

  return sections.join('\n')
}

content.text:
Here's a summary of everything that was created:

## Package: `@prisma/generator-rest-client`

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package config with `@prisma/generator-helper`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator` as deps; dual CJS/ESM exports; `prisma-generator-rest-client` binary |
| `tsconfig.json` | Extends root `tsconfig.json` |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json` for build output |
| `helpers/build.ts` | esbuild config using `adapterConfig` (CJS+ESM) plus a separate bin entry |
| `src/index.ts` | **Entry point** — implements `generatorHandler` with `onManifest` and `onGenerate` callbacks; writes the generated `rest-client.ts` to the configured output directory |
| `src/generator.ts` | **Core generation logic** — parses DMMF models/enums and generates: model interfaces, `CreateInput`/`UpdateInput`/`WhereUniqueInput` types, per-model CRUD client classes (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`), and a root `RestClient` compositor |
| `src/template.ts` | **Template helpers** — Prisma-to-TS type mapping, list/nullable wrapping, JSDoc generation, file header |
| `src/bin.ts` | Binary entry point (shebang + imports `./index`) |
| `src/__tests__/generator.test.ts` | **19 tests** covering type mapping, field type composition, interface generation, CRUD methods, enum generation, relation field exclusion |

### Also modified
- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — added `@prisma/generator-rest-client` path mapping for editor go-to-definition

### Verification
- ✅ **Build succeeds** — produces `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/index.d.mts`, `dist/bin.js`
- ✅ **All 19 tests pass**

### Usage in a Prisma schema
```prisma
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```

This generates a typed `rest-client.ts` with interfaces and a `RestClient` class whose properties (e.g., `.user`, `.post`) each have `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, and `delete` methods using `fetch`.

```prisma
generator restClient {
```
